Calculate LD matrix for BRIER.S

calLD.Rd

Compute a sparse LD reference matrix from a genotype reference panel, with optional soft-thresholding and block-structured LD pruning. Used as input to BRIERs.

Usage

calLD(X, sumstats, LDB = NULL, tau = 0)

Arguments

X

An n x p reference genotype matrix (individual-level).

sumstats

A data.frame of marginal summary statistics. Required when LDB is supplied; must contain CHR, BP, and corr columns.

LDB

Optional m x 3 matrix of LD block boundaries (chr, start, stop), e.g. from Berisa et al. If NULL, the full p x p LD matrix is computed.

tau

A numeric scalar. Soft-threshold parameter applied to LD entries; entries with |LD| < tau are set to zero. Defaults to 0 (no thresholding).

Value

An object of class "LD" containing:

XtX

The p x p sparse LD matrix.

blk

The block boundary vector (NULL if no LDB supplied).

nz

Integer vector of non-constant column indices retained.

See also

BRIERs, p2cor

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# full LD matrix
ld <- calLD(X, tau = 0.05)

# block-structured LD with Berisa et al. blocks
ld <- calLD(X, sumstats = sumstats, LDB = LDB, tau = 0.05)
} # }
